task_completion.description: The Volume Rendering module is opened and volume rendering is enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Volume Rendering' module is selected in the 3D Slicer GUI, and the visibility toggle (eye icon) next to the volume is turned on.

task_completion.description: A bone volume rendering preset is applied to the volume.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A bone-specific preset (e.g., 'CT-Bone') is selected or highlighted in the Display -> Preset section of the Volume Rendering module.

task_completion.description: Skeletal structures are successfully visualized in the 3D view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer displays a rendered 3D volume showing distinct skeletal structures (bones) rather than opaque soft tissue.

task_completion.description: The spine is clearly visible and focused in the 3D view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view is oriented, zoomed, or cropped such that the spine is prominently displayed, unobstructed, and clearly identifiable.

task_completion.description: The agent captures a screenshot of the 3D view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Screen Capture module is open, or a 'Save Data' / screenshot dialog is visible on the screen, specifically targeting the 3D view.

task_completion.description: The screenshot is saved to the exact requested file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog, Screen Capture module, or terminal shows the file being saved exactly to '~/Documents/SlicerData/Screenshots/bone_rendering.png'.

integrity.description: The agent used the 3D Slicer GUI to perform the rendering and capture the screenshot.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the agent clicking through 3D Slicer menus, modules, and the 3D view, rather than just executing a background script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The rendered image is genuinely produced within 3D Slicer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D rendering appears within the 3D Slicer application window, complete with standard Slicer UI elements like the 3D view axis and orientation markers.

integrity.description: The agent is working on the correct pre-loaded dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Data module, Volume Rendering module, or volume selector dropdown shows 'amos_0001' as the active volume.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses the AMOS 2022 dataset with case ID amos_0001. The data is synthetic, generated by a script that simulates Hounsfield Units for soft tissue (40 HU), spine (500 HU), and aorta (200 HU). The VLM should verify that the rendered spine is consistent with a CT scan of an abdominal region.
